name: Bond and Equity Returns

book_quotes.quote: Figure 10.10 displays 1926-2010 average annual stock market and corporate and government bond rates of return, which are generally quite different (Ibbotson 2004, 30-31, table 2-2). These are nominal rates, but subtracting a common rate of inflation from each to make them into real rates would obviously not change the evident difference between the three sets.
Source: Ibbotson Associates, SBBI Valuation Edition 2004 Yearbook, 30-31, table 2-2.
Rates of return on large company stocks and long-term corporate and government bonds for 1926-2003 are from Ibbotson (2004, 30-31, table 2-2). I thank David Stubbs for having updated these to 2010.

primary_source.publication: Ibbotson SBBI 2004 Valuation Yearbook, Table 2-2 (pp.30-31); updated to 2010 by David Stubbs
primary_source.table_or_series_id: rslarge (large-company stocks), rbcorplt (long-term corporate bonds), rbgovlt (long-term govt bonds)
primary_source.license: Commercial (Morningstar/Ibbotson SBBI subscription); historical Yearbook reproductions in academic libraries
primary_source.replaced_by: Morningstar Direct now publishes Ibbotson SBBI annual data under license

formula: rslarge_t, rbcorplt_t, rbgovlt_t = nominal annual total returns from Ibbotson table 2-2

components.label: Long-Term Corporate Bonds total return
components.source: Ibbotson SBBI 2004, table 2-2 (~20yr AAA/AA corporate index)

extension_candidates.subseries_label: Damodaran historical equity & bond return tables (publicly available reconstruction)
concerns: Damodaran's series largely replicates Ibbotson methodology and is free; small definitional differences in corporate bond index

extension_candidates.source: S&P Dow Jones Indices / Bloomberg / FRED (SP500 proxy)
concerns: FRED SP500 is price index, not total return; need TR series from S&P directly

methodology_notes: Ibbotson SBBI Yearbook is the canonical US asset-class return series; widely used in finance and pension actuarial work.
David Stubbs extension 2004-2010 acknowledged in Appendix 10.1; methodology consistent with Ibbotson original.
Table 10.1 averages (1926-2010): Large Stocks 11.88%, LT Corporate Bonds 6.24%, LT Govt Bonds 5.91% - these are the equity-premium-puzzle numbers Shaikh discusses.
Nominal returns; Shaikh deliberately does NOT real-deflate because his point (a common inflation deflator wouldn't change rank ordering).
open_questions: Ibbotson is now a commercial product (Morningstar). Phase 4: confirm whether RSCD can use Damodaran's free reconstruction as the modern source or must licence Ibbotson.
Should the extension stop at the last freely-available Ibbotson year (2010) or attempt to splice Damodaran for 2011+?

review_history.notes: CD2 S056 ported; Ibbotson licensing flagged; Damodaran proposed as open extension
